REVUE
12 // Get orders for selected users
13 const enrichedUsers = await Promise.all(
14 users.map(async (user) => {
- const orders = await db.orders.findOne({
- where: { userId: user.id }
- });
+ // Batch fetch outside loop instead
18 return { ...user, orders };
19 })
20 );
+ const allUserIds = users.map(u => u.id);
+ const allOrders = await db.orders.findMany({
+ where: { userId: { in: allUserIds } }
+ });
REVUE SUGGESTION

N+1 query pattern detected. Executing findOne inside a loop creates N database calls. Replace with findMany using { in: [...] } before the loop.

Est. gain: 87% latency reduction.

detectPatterns()|

Pattern Recognition.

REVUE learns from your codebase's existing patterns before touching a single PR. Suggestions match how your team writes code, not how a generic model thinks code should be written.

assessRisk(change)|

Risk Classification.

High-risk changes (auth, payments, migrations) get a different review tier. REVUE knows the difference between a CSS fix and a payment flow change.

checkContext(pr, codebase)|

Codebase Context.

Every suggestion links back to a specific file or function in your repository that establishes precedent. Not opinion — evidence.

generateComment(issue)|

Human-Readable Output.

Review comments read like they were written by a thoughtful colleague, not generated by a model that has never merged a PR.

app/tasks/processor.py | feat/background-jobs
REVUE
42async def process_batch(job_ids: List[str]):
43 results = {}
44 for job_id in job_ids:
- # Fetch job status from external API
- result = fetch_job_result(job_id)
- results[job_id] = result
+ # Parallelize IO bound fetching
+ tasks = [fetch_job_result(jid) for jid in job_ids]
+ resolved = await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
+ results = dict(zip(job_ids, resolved))
51 return results
REVUE ANALYSIS

Missing await on fetch_job_result(). This assigns the coroutine object, not its result. results[job_id] will contain <coroutine object>.

Replaced with asyncio.gather() to also parallelize — current sequential approach adds ~200ms latency per job.
